text:
*[Bug 17549] When building a package, use inRbuildignore() before copying package contents to Tdir*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17549>

--- Comment #8 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
Yes. Bug 18186 was about broken symlinks, even when in `.Rbuildignore`,
preventing `R CMD build` from working. With r90095, we get:

```
setwd(tempdir())
package.skeleton('foo', list = character())
file.symlink('/does/not/exist', 'foo/broken_symlink')
# [1] TRUE
tools::Rcmd('build foo') # fails, as it should
# * checking for file ‘foo/DESCRIPTION’ ... OK
#  ERROR
# copying to build directory failed
writeLines('^broken_symlink$', 'foo/.Rbuildignore')
tools::Rcmd('build foo') # now succeeds, as desired
# * checking for file ‘foo/DESCRIPTION’ ... OK
# * preparing ‘foo’:
# * checking DESCRIPTION meta-information ... OK
# * installing the package (it is needed to process help pages)
# * saving partial Rd database
# * checking for LF line-endings in source and make files and shell scripts
# * checking for empty or unneeded directories
# * building ‘foo_1.0.tar.gz’
```

(Previously confirmed by Jennifer Bryan in Bug 18186 Comment 7.)

text:
*[Bug 19082] New: `windows()` keyboard events doesn't correctly handle "Esc", "Backspace", "Tab", "Enter", Ctrl+M, F11, F12*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19082>

            Bug ID: 19082
           Summary: `windows()` keyboard events doesn't correctly handle
                    "Esc", "Backspace", "Tab", "Enter", Ctrl+M, F11, F12
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Windows 64-bit
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Windows GUI / Window specific
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:trevor.l.davis@gmail.com|trevor.l.davis@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3720
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3720&action=edit>
Correctly handle Esc, Backspace, Tab, Enter, Ctrl+M, F11, F12

The following code helps test keyboard events in interactive x11() and
windows() devices:

```
keystroke <- function(key) {
    cp <- utf8ToInt(key)
    if (length(cp) == 1L) {
        if (grepl("[[:space:][:cntrl:]]", key)) {
            cat(sprintf("keystroke   (U+%04X)\n", cp))
        } else {
            cat(sprintf("keystroke   key=%s  (U+%04X)\n", key, cp))
        }
    } else {
        cat(sprintf("keystroke   key=%s\n", key))
    }
    NULL
}
if (identical(.Platform$OS.type, "unix")) {
    x11(type = "Xlib")
} else {
    windows()
}
par(mar = c(0, 0, 0, 0))
plot.new()
getGraphicsEvent(prompt = "Press keys to test (close window to quit)",
                 onKeybd = keystroke)
```

I observe the following issues when testing the keyboard events in `windows()`:

1. "Esc", "Backspace", "Tab", and "Enter" currently return "ctrl-" values that
match and obscure actual "ctrl-" values and don't match what `x11()` returns. 
For example "Tab" currently returns "ctrl-I" which is also what Control-I also
returns whereas `x11()` returns `"\t"`.
2. Currently F11 and F12 don't emit an event on `windows()` while they do on
`x11()`
3. Currently Control-M returns "ctrl-J" instead of "ctrl-M" whereas on `x11()`
it returns "ctrl-M".

A summary of proposed changes as a table:

| Key | `windows()` pre-patch | `windows()` post-patch | `x11()` |
|---|---|---|---|
| Esc | `"ctrl-["` | `"\x1b"` | `"\x1b"` |
| Ctrl+[ | `"ctrl-["` | `"ctrl-["` | `"ctrl-{"` |
| Backspace | `"ctrl-H"` | `"\b"` | `"\b"` |
| Ctrl+H | `"ctrl-H"` | `"ctrl-H"` | `"ctrl-H"` |
| Tab | `"ctrl-I"` | `"\t"` | `"\t"` |
| Ctrl+I | `"ctrl-I"` | `"ctrl-I"` | `"ctrl-I"` |
| Enter | `"ctrl-J"` | `"\r"` | `"\r"` |
| Ctrl+J | `"ctrl-J"` | `"ctrl-J"` | `"ctrl-J"` |
| Ctrl+M | `"ctrl-J"` | `"ctrl-M"` | `"ctrl-M"` |
| F11 | (dropped) | `"F11"` | `"F11"` |
| F12 | (dropped) | `"F12"` | `"F12"` |

Note as a side effect of this patch you may no longer use Control-M as an alias
for Enter and Control-J for entering newlines in the R GUI.  I'm not entirely
sure who (if any) uses Control-M instead of the Enter key (or Control-J) to
press enter in the R GUI but theoretically this may be considered a breaking
change.

Note if you look at the Control+[ line that `x11()` actually returns `"ctrl-{"`
instead of `"ctrl-["`.  It seems this is because in `x11()` Control characters
currently always get a "Shift" applied to them so for example Control+a becomes
"ctrl-A" and Control+2 becomes "ctrl-@".

text:
*[Bug 19080] getGenerics() can return package names in addition to generics*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19080>

Mikael Jagan (<mailto:jaganmn2@gmail.com|jaganmn2@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |jaganmn2@gmail.com

--- Comment #3 from Mikael Jagan (<mailto:jaganmn2@gmail.com|jaganmn2@gmail.com>) ---
Before Martin's reply, I spent a moment looking at the patch (attachment 3718).
 I would prefer a test limited to the running process, being more
contained/expository.  Can we not just modify and revert `.genericTable` and
test in between?

```
gT <- methods:::.genericTable
gnm <- "zzz"
pnm <- c("pkg1", "pkg2")
gF <- sapply(pnm,
             function(.) makeGeneric(gnm, fdefault = identity, package = .),
             simplify = FALSE)
str(gF, max.level = 3L)
stopifnot(vapply(gF, is, FALSE, "genericFunction"),
          vapply(gF, slot, "", "generic") == gnm,
          vapply(gF, slot, "", "package") == pnm)
gT[[gnm]] <- gF
owp <- getGenerics()
stopifnot(is(owp, "ObjectsWithPackage"),
          (m <- match(pnm, owp@package, 0L)) > 0L,
          owp[m] == gnm) # previously, owp[m] == pnm
gT[[gnm]] <- NULL
```

Aside: `str(z <- getGenerics())` shows that `getGenerics` can set `names(z)`
and `names(z@package)`.  These attributes seem superfluous, and they are not
mentioned by `help("ObjectsWithPackage-class")`.  Could they be a kludge to
help with subsetting?  In that case, maybe **methods** should do
`setMethod("[", c(x = "ObjectsWithPackage"), ...)`.

text:
*[Bug 19081] `x11()` keyboard events doesn't correctly handle "Home", "Delete", and Keypad when Num Lock is off*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19081>

--- Comment #1 from Trevor Davis (<mailto:trevor.l.davis@gmail.com|trevor.l.davis@gmail.com>) ---
Created attachment 3721
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3721&action=edit>
Fix CTRL keyboard events for non-letters

While working on bug#19082 I noticed another bug in `x11()` keyboard event
handling: when combining a CONTROL with a non-letter character it would always
be "shifted" i.e. CONTROL+[ would return "Ctrl-{" instead of "Ctrl-[".  This
patch should fix this.

Here are some examples of how behaviour changes pre/post patch:

| Key (US keyboard) | `x11()` pre-patch | `x11()` post-patch |
|---|---|---|
| Ctrl+A -- Ctrl+Z | `"ctrl-A"` -- `"ctrl-Z"` | `"ctrl-A"`-- `"ctrl-Z"` |
| Ctrl+1 | `"ctrl-!"` | `"ctrl-1"` |
| Ctrl+2 | `"ctrl-@"` | `"ctrl-2"` |
| Ctrl+Shift+2 | `"ctrl-@"` | `"ctrl-@"` |
| Ctrl+[ | `"ctrl-{"` | `"ctrl-["` |
| Ctrl+Shift+[ | `"ctrl-{"` | `"ctrl-{"`|

*[Bug 18874] Nested grid viewports with null produce incorrect grob rendering on small windows*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18874>

--- Comment #3 from Paul Murrell (<mailto:paul@stat.auckland.ac.nz|paul@stat.auckland.ac.nz>) ---
Created attachment 3722
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3722&action=edit>
patch for possible fix

This may just be a thinko (returning a TRUE instead of a FALSE - see the
suggested patch attached).

This simple change produces more defensible behaviour for the example in the
original report.

I will follow up with example code that I think demonstrates the old bug
behaviour and the new better behaviour more clearly.

text:
*[Bug 18874] Nested grid viewports with null produce incorrect grob rendering on small windows*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18874>

--- Comment #4 from Paul Murrell (<mailto:paul@stat.auckland.ac.nz|paul@stat.auckland.ac.nz>) ---
Created attachment 3723
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3723&action=edit>
demonstration of problem and fix

This code demonstrates layouts within a parent viewport that is smaller than
the page/window, so it is possible to see the layout extending beyond the
parent when the absolute size of the layout exceeds the absolute size of the
parent.

The basic idea is that absolute layout widths/heights are allocated regardless
of the size of the parent, so may extend beyond the parent (with the layout
justification controlling how that extension occurs), BUT relative
widths/heights should be zero if there is no width/height left after allocating
the absolute widths/heights.

In the nested case, the parent has zero width (in these examples), so the
absolute widths exceed this parent (details controlled by justification) and
the relative widths are zero.

The PROBLEM examples are all fixed by the patch suggested in the previous
comment.

If it is agreed that the patch behaviour is an improvement, further testing is
required, at least involving regression testing to ensure that the patch does
not break any existing behaviour, especially in {ggplot2} and {lattice}.

elements.text: [Bug 18874] Nested grid viewports with null produce incorrect grob rendering on small windows

demonstration of problem and fix

This code demonstrates layouts within a parent viewport that is smaller than
the page/window, so it is possible to see the layout extending beyond the
parent when the absolute size of the layout exceeds the absolute size of the
parent.

The basic idea is that absolute layout widths/heights are allocated regardless
of the size of the parent, so may extend beyond the parent (with the layout
justification controlling how that extension occurs), BUT relative
widths/heights should be zero if there is no width/height left after allocating
the absolute widths/heights.

In the nested case, the parent has zero width (in these examples), so the
absolute widths exceed this parent (details controlled by justification) and
the relative widths are zero.

The PROBLEM examples are all fixed by the patch suggested in the previous
comment.

If it is agreed that the patch behaviour is an improvement, further testing is
required, at least involving regression testing to ensure that the patch does
not break any existing behaviour, especially in {ggplot2} and {lattice}.

text:
*[Bug 19080] getGenerics() can return package names in addition to generics*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19080>

--- Comment #4 from Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) ---
My own preference is to write tests on the public API. An earlier version of
the patch indeed edited .genericTable, but that's just an implementation
detail, and thus to be avoided following, e.g.

<https://stackoverflow.com/questions/105007/should-i-test-private-methods-or-only-public-ones>

<https://www.ibm.com/think/insights/unit-testing-best-practices>

<https://enterprisecraftsmanship.com/posts/unit-testing-private-methods/>

Of course, maybe we're exposing a need for a new piece of public code that
would help in contexts besides just unit testing, or would help write unit
tests for other S4 packages.

(admittedly, my current test _does_ **use** the private API, but the "actual
test logic", being done in the subprocess, does manage to be more hermetic. if
pushed I think we could run package.skeleton itself on a subprocess and avoid
this)
